echo "=== DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig - full implementation ==="
cat pii-reporting-api/src/main/java/pro/softcom/aisentinel/infrastructure/confluence/adapter/out/config/DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig.java

# Check ConfluenceUrlInitializer to see what type 'config' is
echo -e "\n=== ConfluenceUrlInitializer - see config type ==="

Repository: Softcom-Technologies-Organization/ai-sentinel

</details>

**Add `@Order` annotations to guarantee cache invalidation completes before URL refresh.**

Two `@EventListener` methods handle `ConfluenceConfigUpdatedEvent`: `DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig.onConfigUpdated()` invalidates the cache, and `ConfluenceUrlInitializer.onConfigUpdated()` reads `config.baseUrl()`. Without explicit ordering, Spring does not guarantee execution order. If the URL refresh runs first, `config.baseUrl()` returns stale cached data because the cache is cleared afterward.

Add `@Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE)` to `DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig.onConfigUpdated()` and `@Order(Ordered.LOWEST_PRECEDENCE)` to `ConfluenceUrlInitializer.onConfigUpdated()` to ensure cache invalidation completes before the URL is refreshed.

<details>
<summary>ðŸ¤– Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@pii-reporting-api/src/main/java/pro/softcom/aisentinel/infrastructure/confluence/adapter/out/config/ConfluenceUrlInitializer.java`
around lines 27 - 32, Add explicit event listener ordering so cache invalidation
runs before URL refresh: annotate
DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig.onConfigUpdated(...) with
`@Order`(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) and annotate
ConfluenceUrlInitializer.onConfigUpdated(...) with
`@Order`(Ordered.LOWEST_PRECEDENCE); ensure the Ordered import
(org.springframework.core.Ordered) and the `@Order` annotation
(org.springframework.core.annotation.Order) are present so Spring guarantees
DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig.invalidates the cache before
ConfluenceUrlInitializer.refreshes the ConfluenceUrlBuilder.
```

</details>

**Fix port mismatch: docker-compose-db.yml uses 5433 but application configs expect 5435.**

The application configurations (application.yml and application-integration.yml) default to DB_PORT=5435, and docker-compose.dev.yml correctly uses port 5435. However, docker-compose-db.yml still maps port 5433:5432, which will cause connection failures when using that standalone database file. Update docker-compose-db.yml line 11 to use 5435:5432 for consistency.

<details>
<summary>ðŸ¤– Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@pii-reporting-api/src/test/resources/application-integration.yml` at line 10,
The docker Compose DB service is exposing port 5433 while application configs
(DB_PORT default) expect 5435; update the port mapping in the DB compose service
from 5433:5432 to 5435:5432 so the environment variable DB_PORT (used in
application-integration.yml / SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL) matches the container
mapping and avoids connection failures.
```

</details>

**Remove these incorrect path mappingsâ€”they don't exist and will cause TypeScript compilation failures.**

The path mappings in lines 10â€“15 reference non-standard locations that do not exist in Angular 21 or PrimeNG 21 packages. Standard Angular and PrimeNG package structures do not include `types/` subdirectories or custom `.d.ts` files at these paths. When `npm install` is run and TypeScript attempts to resolve these imports (e.g., `@angular/core/testing`), it will fail to find the mapped type definition files, breaking test compilation.

Remove these mappings unless custom stub files exist in your project; otherwise, rely on the packages' standard exports which resolve correctly without explicit path mappings.

<details>
<summary>ðŸ¤– Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@pii-reporting-ui/tsconfig.spec.json` around lines 10 - 15, Remove the invalid
path mappings in tsconfig.spec.json for the module keys "@angular/core/testing",
"@angular/common/http", "primeng/multiselect", "primeng/types/multiselect",
"primeng/*", and "@angular/platform-browser/animations" (the entries shown in
the diff); delete these entries unless you actually have corresponding custom
.d.ts stub files in the repo, and rely on the packages' standard exports instead
so TypeScript resolves types from the installed Angular/PrimeNG packages without
custom path overrides.
```

</details>

**Guard detector availability before invoking `detect_pii`.**

Line 702 dereferences `self.detector` unconditionally, but `_create_single_detector()` can return `None` (Line 233-237). In that config, requests fail with `AttributeError` instead of a controlled path.



<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-        entities = self.detector.detect_pii(content, threshold, **call_kwargs)
+        if self.detector is None:
+            logger.warning(
+                "[%s] No detector instance configured; skipping detection for this request",
+                request_id,
+            )
+            return []
+
+        entities = self.detector.detect_pii(content, threshold, **call_kwargs)
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>ðŸ¤– Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```

In
`@pii-detector-service/pii_detector/infrastructure/adapter/in/grpc/pii_service.py`
around lines 699 - 703, Guard against self.detector being None before calling
detect_pii: in the method that currently builds call_kwargs and calls
self.detector.detect_pii (the block that calls _build_detection_kwargs and
detect_pii), check if self.detector is truthy (or call _create_single_detector
to reinitialize) and handle the None case by returning a controlled
error/response or raising a clear exception; reference the factory method
_create_single_detector and the instance attribute self.detector and ensure you
do not call detect_pii when self.detector is None.
```

</details>

<summary>ðŸ¤– Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

around lines 732 - 742, The code currently guards only 'enable_ml' against the

**Avoid raw PII snippets in INFO-level app logs.**

These logs include `text` previews for detected entities at INFO level. That can leak sensitive values into standard operational logs and broaden PII exposure.



<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-        entity_text_preview = entity.get('text', '')[:30]
+        entity_text_len = len(str(entity.get('text', '')))
@@
-            f"text='{entity_text_preview}' | score={entity_score:.3f}"
+            f"text_len={entity_text_len} | score={entity_score:.3f}"
@@
-                f"(disabled in config for detector={config_detector}) | text='{entity_text_preview}'"
+                f"(disabled in config for detector={config_detector})"
@@
-                f"(score {entity_score:.3f} < threshold {type_threshold:.3f}) | "
-                f"text='{entity_text_preview}'"
+                f"(score {entity_score:.3f} < threshold {type_threshold:.3f})"
```
</details>

include raw PII snippets via variables like entity_text_preview, entity_text,
request_id, entity_type_raw, entity_type, entity_type_upper, idx and
entity_score must be changed to avoid exposing sensitive text; replace or redact
the text preview (e.g., remove entity_text_preview/entity_text, replace with a

_âš ï¸� Potential issue_ | _ðŸŸ  Major_

**Do not silently accept plaintext API tokens.**

If `isEncrypted()` is false, the raw token is used as-is. That weakens the encrypted-at-rest guarantee and can hide migration regressions. Prefer fail-closed or explicit remediation (warn + re-encrypt path).

<details>
<summary>ðŸ¤– Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```

`@pii-reporting-api/src/main/java/pro/softcom/aisentinel/infrastructure/confluence/adapter/out/config/DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig.java`
around lines 65 - 67, The current logic in
DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig returns the raw token when
encryptionService.isEncrypted(encrypted) is false; instead fail-closed or
perform explicit remediation: update the method that handles the stored token
(reference encryptionService.isEncrypted and the variable/parameter named
encrypted inside DatabaseBackedConfluenceConnectionConfig) to throw a clear
exception when an unencrypted token is encountered (or alternatively log a
warning and call encryptionService.encrypt(...) to re-encrypt and persist the
token before returning it), ensuring callers never receive plaintext tokens
silently and that any migration path is explicit and auditable.
```

</details>

_âš ï¸� Potential issue_ | _ðŸŸ  Major_

**Validate detector offsets before conversion to avoid hard failures.**

`entity.getStart()` / `entity.getEnd()` come from an external service. If either is negative or beyond code-point length, `offsetByCodePoints` will throw and fail the full page analysis.



<details>
<summary>Proposed hardening</summary>

```diff
-        if (hasSupplementaryChars) {
-            start = codePointIndexToCodeUnitIndex(content, start);
-            end = codePointIndexToCodeUnitIndex(content, end);
-        }
+        if (hasSupplementaryChars) {
+            int maxCodePoints = content.codePointCount(0, content.length());
+            int safeStart = Math.max(0, Math.min(start, maxCodePoints));
+            int safeEnd = Math.max(safeStart, Math.min(end, maxCodePoints));
+            start = codePointIndexToCodeUnitIndex(content, safeStart);
+            end = codePointIndexToCodeUnitIndex(content, safeEnd);
+        }
```
</details>

around lines 112 - 115, The detector offsets from
entity.getStart()/entity.getEnd() may be negative or exceed the code-point
length causing String.offsetByCodePoints (used inside
codePointIndexToCodeUnitIndex) to throw; before converting when
hasSupplementaryChars is true, validate and clamp these offsets to the range [0,

**Remove redundant Enter handler to avoid duplicate connection tests.**

Both `(click)` and `(keydown.enter)` can fire for keyboard activation before the `[disabled]` binding takes effect, causing duplicate HTTP requests and duplicate success/error messages. PrimeNG buttons handle Enter key activation through the click handler alone; the explicit `(keydown.enter)` binding is redundant.

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
         <p-button
           type="button"
           [label]="'settings.confluence.buttons.test' | transloco"
           icon="pi pi-wifi"
           [loading]="testing()"
           [disabled]="testing() || saving()"
           (click)="onTestConnection()"
-          (keydown.enter)="onTestConnection()"
           styleClass="p-button-outlined">
         </p-button>
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion

          styleClass="p-button-outlined">
```

<!-- suggestion_end -->

button is redundant and can cause duplicate calls to onTestConnection(); remove
the (keydown.enter)="onTestConnection()" attribute from the button markup so the
PrimeNG button's native click activation (which already handles Enter) invokes
onTestConnection() once, and keep the existing [disabled] binding in place to
prevent triggering when disabled.
```

ðŸŒ� Web query:

`RxJS 7.8.x forkJoin empty array behavior next emission`

ðŸ’¡ Result:

In **RxJS 7.8.x**, `forkJoin`:

- **`forkJoin([])` (empty input array)** returns an observable that **completes immediately and does not emit (`next`) anything** (i.e., youâ€™ll only get `complete`). [1][2]
- If **any inner observable completes without emitting** (e.g. `EMPTY`), `forkJoin([...])` **completes without emitting** (no `next`), even if other sources already produced values. [1]

If you need a `next` emission for the empty-array case, handle it explicitly, e.g.:

```ts
const result$ = sources.length ? forkJoin(sources) : of([]);
```

Or force an emission downstream:

```ts
forkJoin(sources).pipe(defaultIfEmpty([]))
```

**Sources:** [1] [2]

ðŸŒ� Web query:

`PrimeNG p-button Enter key click dispatch behavior`

ðŸ’¡ Result:

- **Keyboard activation is native browser behavior**: when a real button is focused, pressing **Enter** (and Space) triggers the elementâ€™s **`click` event** (i.e., the browser dispatches a â€œclick-equivalentâ€� activation). [1]

- **PrimeNG `<p-button>`**: the component exposes an **`onClick`** output (not Angularâ€™s native `(click)`), and it has a **`type` input whose default is `"button"`**â€”so it **wonâ€™t submit a form unless you set `type="submit"`**. [2]

- **PrimeNG `pButton` directive on a native `<button>`** (`<button pButton ...>`): the underlying HTML rule appliesâ€”**a `<button>` with no `type` defaults to `type="submit"`**, so pressing Enter (often from an input in the same form, depending on browser â€œimplicit submitâ€� rules) can submit the form / trigger the submit button unless you set `type="button"`. [3]

**Practical takeaway**
- Use `<p-button (onClick)="..." type="button">` for non-submit actions. [2]  
- Use `<p-button type="submit">` (or a native `<button type="submit" pButton>`) when you *want* Enter-to-submit. [2][3]

**Sources**  
[1] MDN â€” `click` event includes keyboard activation (Enter/Space) when focused: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/click_event  
[2] PrimeNG source (`p-button`): `@Input() type: string = 'button'` and `@Output() onClick`: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/primefaces/primeng/master/packages/primeng/src/button/button.ts  
[3] Webhint docs â€” default `<button>` type is `submit` unless specified: https://webhint.io/docs/user-guide/hints/hint-button-type/

ðŸŒ� Web query:

`PrimeNG 21 MultiSelectChangeEvent value null array clear documentation`

ðŸ’¡ Result:

In PrimeNG **MultiSelect**, the `(onChange)` payload type is:

- `export interface MultiSelectChangeEvent { originalEvent: Event; value: any; itemValue?: any; }`

So `event.value` is **not guaranteed to be an array**â€”it can be `null` (e.g., after a clear). [1]

When you enable the clear icon (`[showClear]="true"`), PrimeNG explicitly supports clearing via a dedicated event:

- `onClear: EventEmitter<void>` (fires when the value is cleared) [2]  
- â€œWhen showClear is enabled, a clear icon is displayed to clear the value.â€� [3]

### Practical handling
```ts
onMsChange(e: MultiSelectChangeEvent) {
  this.selected = e.value ?? [];   // treat null as â€œclearedâ€�
}

onMsClear() {
  this.selected = [];              // explicit clear path
}
```

**Sources:**  
[1] PrimeNG `MultiSelectChangeEvent` definition (UNPKG) ([app.unpkg.com](https://app.unpkg.com/primeng%4017.1.0/files/multiselect/multiselect.interface.d.ts?utm_source=openai))  
[2] PrimeNG `MultiSelect` declares `onClear: EventEmitter<void>` (UNPKG) ([app.unpkg.com](https://app.unpkg.com/primeng%4019.0.8/files/multiselect/multiselect.d.ts?utm_source=openai))  
[3] PrimeNG MultiSelect docs (`showClear` behavior) ([primeng.org](https://primeng.org/multiselect?utm_source=openai))
